title: eriogonum annuum subsp. hitchcockii
intro: Eriogonum annuum subsp. hitchcockii, also known as Hitchcock's buckwheat or desert buckwheat, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the buckwheat family that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant is used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Eriogonum annuum subsp. hitchcockii include its ability to reduce fever, soothe skin conditions, and support immune function. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is also used in traditional folk remedies for its purported health benefits.
